retrieved_docs.title: Histology, Staining -- Tissue Preparation
retrieved_docs.content: Antigen Retrieval: This step is to retrieve antigens that could have been covered in the fixation and embedding stages. If the cross-linking of proteins conceals the antigen sites, there may not be as robust of an immunohistochemical response. Antigen retrieval is achieved through heating and proteolytic methods to break down the cross-links and reveal the epitopes and antigens that were previously covered. [1] Although this step carries the risk of denaturing both the fixative and the antigens themselves, a successful antigen retrieval method can lead to a much more effective immunostaining intensity.

retrieved_docs.title: Histology, Staining -- Tissue Preparation
retrieved_docs.content: Fixation: Fixation uses chemicals to preserve the structure of the tissue in its natural form and protects it from degradation by irreversibly cross-linking proteins. Although several specialized fixatives are available, Neutral Buffered Formalin is a common choice for this step. The fixation step is vital to the rest of the histologic staining procedure because by retaining the chemical composition of the tissue, the sample is hardened and makes the sectioning phase easier. Paraffin-formalin is another effective fixative. Its benefit is that it is the fixative of choice for immunostaining; however, it requires preparation at the time of the fixation. Bouin is a fixative used for examining embryo and brain tissue because of its superior preservation of delicate nuclei and glycogen. Its downside is that it does not preserve kidney tissues well and also distorts mitochondrial structure. [1]

retrieved_docs.title: Histology, Kidney and Glomerulus -- Microscopy, Light -- Immunofluorescent Microscopy
retrieved_docs.content: Immunofluorescent protocols generally call for tissue to be frozen rather than fixed. Fixation of tissue does not completely eliminate the possibility of performing immunofluorescence; however, it is not as ideal. Cross-linking fixatives, such as formalin and glutaraldehyde, are excellent at preserving morphologic integrity but may cross-link target antigens and introduce too much background autofluorescence. If the biopsy is being performed near the lab doing the procedure, then the tissue can be transported in regular saline. If the sample needs to travel further, then a medium such as Michel medium is preferred.  Sectioning can occur on a cryostat with 2 to 4 μM sections. Again, the standard workup is institution-dependent; however, routine examination typically includes IgG, IgM, IgA, complement proteins (C3, C1q, C4), fibrin, and kappa/gamma light chains along with appropriate controls. [20]

retrieved_docs.title: Collagen Cross Linking for Keratoconus -- Technique or Treatment -- Epithelial On Collagen Cross Linking
retrieved_docs.content: In this technique, the corneal epithelium is not scraped, and cross-linking is performed with epithelium. In the beginning, various studies showed limited riboflavin diffusion through the tight epithelial junctions, thus reducing the effectiveness of CXL. [26] However, this technique offered the advantage of no risk for keratitis, postoperative pain, and persistent epithelial defect. This problem has been overcome by adding chemicals that cause epithelial disruption, such as surfactant benzalkonium chloride (BAC) or ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid (EDTA). [27]

retrieved_docs.title: Histology, Skin -- Tissue Preparation
retrieved_docs.content: After completion of a skin biopsy, the tissue is generally processed and sectioned for analysis with light microscopy. Skin tissue processing methods vary among laboratories; however, the paraffin technique is the most common. The standard steps for this process are fixation, dehydration, clearing, and paraffin infiltration. Fresh tissue is generally stored and transferred in 10% neutral buffered formalin fixative, which helps maintain tissue architecture by cross-linking lysine residues. Specimens get embedded in wax before mounting on glass slides for viewing. However, wax is not soluble in water or alcohol; therefore the specimens must undergo dehydration first. Alcohol dehydration is used to remove water from the sample. Alcohol is then removed by xylene as they are miscible in a process referred to as "clearing." The tissue is placed in a warm paraffin wax which replaces the spaces which water previously occupied. After the blocks have cooled, they are cut into thin slices, rehydrated with water, and then stained with stains such as hematoxylin and eosin. The amount of time for this process is dependent on tissue size, temperature, and reagents used.

retrieved_docs.title: Histology, Meissner Corpuscle -- Tissue Preparation
retrieved_docs.content: Meissner corpuscles can be identified on skin biopsy specimens under traditional light microscopy. Immediately after excision, the biopsied specimen is placed into a fixative solution of neutral buffered formalin that forms crosslinks between lysine residues to preserve tissue structure. The specimen is then placed into a small cassette before being infiltrated by paraffin. After the wax cools and hardens, the tissue-containing paraffin block is sliced into thin sections by a microtome. The sections may then be stained according to previously-described protocols for hematoxylin and eosin staining, immunohistochemistry, or immunofluorescence.

retrieved_docs.title: Histology, Periosteum And Endosteum -- Tissue Preparation -- Transmission Electron Microscopy (TEM)
retrieved_docs.content: Cut the tissue into small samples (approximately1 mm) Fix into 2% paraformaldehyde and 0.5% gluteradehyde. Embed in the acrylate- and methacrylate-based resin Lowicryl HM23 with descending temperature according to standard protocol.

retrieved_docs.title: Histology, Alveolar Macrophages -- Tissue Preparation -- Fixation
retrieved_docs.content: Formalin fixation with paraffin embedding: Lung tissue is often fixed in 10% neutral-buffered formalin to preserve alveolar macrophage architecture. The tissue is dehydrated and embedded in paraffin for sectioning, which is suitable for long-term preservation and immunohistochemical analysis. [29] Cryopreservation: In cases where preserving specific protein markers is necessary for studies like flow cytometry or immunofluorescence, tissues may be flash-frozen in optimal cutting temperature compound without formalin fixation. This step allows for immediate sectioning and analysis while maintaining functional proteins and cell surface markers. Glutaraldehyde fixation: Glutaraldehyde is a suitable fixative for high-resolution ultrastructural studies using electron microscopy. Glutaraldehyde preserves intracellular organelles, such as phagosomes and lysosomes, which is crucial for studying the phagocytic activity of alveolar macrophages. [30]

retrieved_docs.title: Collagen Cross Linking for Keratoconus -- Continuing Education Activity
retrieved_docs.content: Objectives: Describe the common clinical and topographic features of keratoconus. Summarize the mechanism of action of collagen crosslinking. Outline the indications for the collagen crosslinking procedure. Review potential outcomes of the procedure in the available literature. Access free multiple choice questions on this topic.

retrieved_docs.title: Collagen Cross Linking for Keratoconus -- Equipment
retrieved_docs.content: The various equipment and instruments needed to perform CXL are Conjunctival forceps Diluted absolute alcohol Calliper Ink pen marker Hockey stick blade Crescent blade Riboflavin Ultraviolet light Hand-held slit lamp Normal saline Saline cannula In the case of accelerated CXL, an accelerated CXL machine is needed. [21]

retrieved_docs.title: Histology, Myelin -- Tissue Preparation
retrieved_docs.content: Historically, an aldehyde fixation was used to fix the nervous tissue for use in electron microscopy. Today, histological fixing of the nervous tissue is commonly done via a high-pressure freezing technique which yields a better-contrasted specimen as well as better preservation of the nervous tissue with fewer artifacts due to better fixation. However, aldehydes, such as formalin, are still used as a fixation solution for purposes of light microscopy as the decreased contrast, and inferior preservation of the tissue is negligible for use in routine nervous tissue studies. [5] [6]

retrieved_docs.title: Histology, Schwann Cells -- Tissue Preparation
retrieved_docs.content: Aldehyde is the preferred routine fixative for nervous tissue. Electron microscopy requires an aldehyde fixative with high purity. [6] After fixation, the sample gets embedded in either paraffin or epoxy. Paraffin allows for the study of the entire cross-sectional area of a nerve and is the preferred medium for light microscopy and larger nerve sections. Epoxy is preferable for smaller nerve branches and visualization with electron microscopy. [7] Recent utilization of cryofixation, high pressure freezing and freeze substitution, is a beneficial supplement to aldehyde fixation in electron microscopy and may improve the preservation of structure detail and contrast. [8]

retrieved_docs.title: Collagen Cross Linking for Keratoconus -- Technique or Treatment -- Epithelial On Collagen Cross Linking
retrieved_docs.content: Wollensak and Iomdina conducted a study using iso-osmolar riboflavin (20% dextran) and BAC, which resulted in biochemical and biomechanical changes after cross-linking in comparison to the control group. However, studies have shown that CXL without epithelial scrape and using BAC containing proparacaine eye drops causes biomechanical stiffening, approximately one-fifth of that induced by Dresden protocol. [18] Another study of porcine eyes comparing epi-off versus epi-on showed that epi-on was 70 % less effective. Although few studies have shown promising results, the efficacy of epi-on is still controversial, and few studies have shown increased keratometry values and retreatment necessity in many cases. [28]

retrieved_docs.title: Histology, Bone -- Tissue Preparation
retrieved_docs.content: Performing undecalcified bone histology varies from the techniques utilized in conventional paraffin embedded methods; this is because of density and lower permeability of the tissue, demanding more time-consuming fixation and processing times. The tissue is first placed in 10% phosphate-buffered formalin solution. Using a band saw, the specimen is cut to size and then placed in an opaque container, submerged in ascending concentrations of ethanol from 70 to 100% for one week at a time, increasing in ten percent increments. Afterward, the specimen is cleared in butanol for one week. It is imperative that the densities of the tissue and resin be similar., which can be done by using a test specimen beforehand and then modifying it the resin density afterward. Generally, a 2-hydroxyethyl methacrylate based preparation solution is the choice when assessing through light microscopy while methyl methacrylate is for immunohistochemistry.

retrieved_docs.title: Collagen Cross Linking for Keratoconus -- Review Questions
retrieved_docs.content: Access free multiple choice questions on this topic. Click here for a simplified version. Comment on this article.

retrieved_docs.title: Collagen Cross Linking for Keratoconus -- Personnel
retrieved_docs.content: For performing CXL, a combined effort of a team of Ophthalmic surgeons, an assistant OT nurse, mid-level ophthalmic personnel (MLOP), and a dedicated counselor is needed. The surgeon performs the surgery, the assistant nurse helps in patient preparation and provides instruments for the surgery, the MLOP helps in patient movement, and the counselor helps in guiding the patient pre-operative and postoperatively. [22]

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2909
retrieved_docs.content: Delivery by double application of forceps is a obstetric procedure in which the fetal head serves as the direct site of the intervention.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 17 -- Level 4 -- Community 2820
retrieved_docs.content: Cerclage (procedure) is clinically associated with قصور عنق الرحم, a condition in which the uterine cervix is unable to maintain sufficient closure during pregnancy, leading to an increased risk of preterm birth or pregnancy loss. The cerclage technique involves placing a suturing material around the cervix to reinforce its structural integrity, thereby preventing premature cervical dilation. This intervention is employed as a therapeutic measure to manage cervical insufficiency and improve obstetric outcomes for affected patients.

This integrated view aids clinicians in understanding the hierarchy of procedures, selecting appropriate techniques for urethral and other organ repairs, and recognizing the shared technical foundations that underlie successful surgical outcomes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 2 -- Community 657
retrieved_docs.content: Urethrovaginal fixation is a specific form of urethral fixation (تثبيت الإحليل).

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 1895
retrieved_docs.content: Removal of cerclage from cervix is a medical intervention that is directly related to the cervical cerclage procedure (إجراء تطويق عنق الرحم). This step involves extracting the sutures that were placed around the cervix to reinforce cervical competence and prevent premature dilation, and it represents a specific phase within the overall management of cervical insufficiency. Recognizing the connection between the removal of the cerclage and the broader cerclage procedure is essential for appropriate patient care and timing of intervention.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 10 -- Level 1 -- Community 78

Collectively, these relationships illustrate a hierarchical framework in which urethral surgical repairs, bladder management, and related pelvic‑floor and hemostatic procedures are organized by shared anatomical sites (the urinary tract proper), common procedural strategies (plication and suturing), and interlinked classifications, thereby informing comprehensive patient management and clinical decision‑making.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 32 -- Level 1 -- Community 233
retrieved_docs.content: while urethrovaginal fixation represents a broader fixation category that includes the specific urethral fixation “تثبيت الإحليل.” Collectively, these relationships form an integrated taxonomy in which broad procedural categories (female pelvic, vaginal, and urethral surgical repairs) branch into increasingly specialized techniques, all unified by the common morphology “النَّاسُور.” This framework assists clinicians in selecting appropriate surgical strategies, understanding the hierarchical taxonomy of related procedures, and recognizing the shared clinical focus on fistula closure and reconstruction across multiple pelvic and genital organ systems.

This integrated framework supports comprehensive patient assessment, informed surgical planning, and coordinated management of fractures and bone‑density disorders.

Overall, the framework connects urethral reconstructive surgeries, broader plication concepts, and hemorrhage‑control suture plications within a unified system of surgical repair techniques that rely on suturing devices and serve therapeutic, hemostatic, and, in some cases, cosmetic purposes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 2 -- Community 1227
retrieved_docs.content: The skin (kůže) is directly related to the procedure of suturing skin and subcutaneous tissue.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 1447
retrieved_docs.content: Cerclage (procedure) and REPRODUCTIVE HISTORY POOR are both clinically associated with قصور عنق الرحم. قصور عنق الرحم, also known as cervical insufficiency, is a condition in which the cervix is unable to maintain its structural integrity during pregnancy, increasing the risk of premature dilation and preterm delivery. The cerclage procedure involves placing a suturing stitch around the cervix to reinforce it and prevent premature opening, thereby directly addressing the mechanical weakness characteristic of قصور عنق الرحم. Additionally, a poor reproductive history—such as prior miscarriages, preterm births, or other adverse pregnancy outcomes—has been linked to a higher likelihood of developing قصور عنق الرحم, indicating that past obstetric complications can serve as a clinical indicator for this cervical disorder. Recognizing both the therapeutic role of cerclage and the predictive value of reproductive history is essential for early identification and management of patients at risk for قصور عنق الرحم.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 17 -- Level 1 -- Community 228
retrieved_docs.content: underlying pathology. Recognizing these hierarchical and functional connections enables clinicians to select appropriate operative techniques, coordinate multidisciplinary care, and address the full spectrum of pelvic and genital fistulas, from basic device‑assisted suturing to complex reconstructive and fixation surgeries aimed at restoring normal urinary‑tract and vaginal anatomy.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2082
retrieved_docs.content: Crushing injuries or internal injuries are associated with open wounds (جرح مفتوح), indicating that such traumatic forces often result in visible tissue disruption.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 22 -- Level 2 -- Community 907
retrieved_docs.content: encompasses Plication as a specific form. Plication functions as a versatile reparative technique, appearing as Plication of kidney, Ureteroplication, and Suture plication of artery for control of gastric hemorrhage, and is considered an inverse instance of both Surgical repair and genitourinary incontinence procedures. Surgical repair methods are applied in Open repair of kidney or renal pelvis and in renal plication (الرأب الكلوي) for stabilizing renal structures. Renal atrophy (الرأب الكلوي) is categorized under جراحة الكلى, indicating that pathological renal conditions are addressed within this surgical framework. The category Other operations on urinary tract connects Replacement of ureterostomy tube to the parent genitourinary incontinence procedures, highlighting the overlap between urinary tract surgeries and continence management. Cosmetic surgery (جراحة التجميل) co‑occurs with جراحة الكلى and also intersects with bladder treatment (علاج المثانة), a specific genitourinary

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2878
retrieved_docs.content: Female urethrorrhaphy is a surgical procedure that involves the suture of the female perineum, used to repair urethral injuries or defects in women.
